items.detail: Sustained shrinkage of permitted domains without replacement pathways implies the urban-first scaling path or timeline is wrong.
A persistent pattern of serious incidents with restrictive political response caps deployment regardless of engineering progress.

items.condition: Liability shift fails to impair legacy auto insurance economics
items.detail: Durable combined-ratio improvement in personal auto without a frequency narrative, while AV miles rise, weakens the short-exposure leg.

items.condition: Private ownership remains resilient in urban markets
items.detail: Broad residual strength and trip data that show limited substitution undermine the utilization and depreciation channel.

items.condition: Fleet economics do not scale as expected
items.detail: Utilization and pricing collapse ahead of autonomy-driven cost relief slows the shift from ownership to on-demand miles.

items.condition: ADS safety persistently trails credible human baselines
items.detail: Comparable data that do not converge on frequency or severity extend liability and adoption timelines materially.
